content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains an integer N, the length of the array. The second line contains N space‑separated integers A1 … AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line with the minimal possible length of the original array.

sampleTestCases.explanation: First array contains only one distinct value, so the minimal original array length is 1. The second array has distinct values {1,2,3}; starting from [1,2,3] we can copy the prefix [1,2] and paste it at the end to obtain the final array.
All three values are different, therefore the initial array must contain all three.
Four distinct numbers require an initial array of length four.

solution.approach: Every value that appears in the final array must appear at least once in the original array because the copy‑paste operation never creates a new value. Hence the original array must contain all distinct values of A. Conversely, if we start with an array that consists of the distinct values of A in the order of their first appearance, we can obtain A by repeatedly copying single elements (a contiguous subsequence of length 1) and pasting them at the required positions. Therefore the minimal possible length equals the number of distinct elements in A. The solution is to count distinct values for each test case using a hash set (or a boolean array when the value range is small).
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(total N) over all test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(D) where D is the number of distinct values (at most 100000).

code.Python3:
import sys

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.buffer.read().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = int(next(it))
        s = set()
        for _ in range(N):
            s.add(int(next(it)))
        out.append(str(len(s)))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
